title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 Contract · Type-System Trace: Event Handler Errors
summary: A service mixes callback and promise APIs, assumes publish returns a Promise, trusts TypeScript types as runtime validation, and exits before asynchronous close and confirms settle. Planned artifact: Add and verify the event handler errors increment in typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Own connection, channel, return, drain, close, error, blocked, unblocked, cancellation, and handler-error events without unhandled process failure.
Treat delivered bytes, fields, properties, headers, and decoded payload as untrusted runtime data despite TypeScript declarations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Event Handler Errors · Theory Event Handler Errors
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5691 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a typed amqplib client without treating types or publish booleans as broker proof; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Own connection, channel, return, drain, close, error, blocked, unblocked, cancellation, and handler-error events without unhandled process failure.
